name: Kahana Bay
tagline: A quiet crescent of sand tucked into a lush valley — the kind of spot most tourists drive right past.
parking: Small lot off Kamehameha Hwy with additional roadside parking
bestTime: Weekday mornings for near-total solitude; spring and summer for calmer water
talkStory: Kahana means 'cutting' or 'turning point' in Hawaiian, and this bay marks the transition between the windward coast's developed towns and the wild North Shore. The valley behind the beach was a thriving ahupua'a (land division) for centuries, with lo'i kalo (taro fields) fed by Kahana Stream. Locals still fish the shallows with throw nets in the evening the same way their kupuna did. The bay connects to the Kahana Valley trail, one of Oahu's few hikes through a living Hawaiian cultural landscape.
